algorithms.label: Cole-Kripke
algorithms.summary: Epoch-by-epoch rest/activity scoring algorithm commonly used in adult actigraphy studies.
algorithms.description: Cole-Kripke is a classic epoch-by-epoch sleep/rest scoring algorithm.
algorithms.citationText: Cole RJ, Kripke DF, Gruen W, et al. Automatic sleep/wake identification from wrist activity.
algorithms.note: General default for adult actigraphy sleep/rest scoring.
warnings: Cole-Kripke parameters were developed for specific data reduction settings. If the 30-second setting fails for lower-frequency data, the backend falls back to the mean-per-minute setting.

parameterSchema.name: settings
parameterSchema.label: Cole-Kripke data reduction setting
parameterSchema.description: pyActigraphy CK defaults to 30sec_max_non_overlap. Use mean if your file is minute-level or the 30-second setting is not compatible.

parameterSchema.name: threshold
parameterSchema.label: Cole-Kripke threshold
parameterSchema.description: Threshold passed to pyActigraphy CK.

parameterSchema.name: rescoring
parameterSchema.label: Apply Cole-Kripke rescoring
parameterSchema.description: Use pyActigraphy's rescoring option when available.

algorithms.label: Sadeh
algorithms.summary: Epoch-by-epoch rest/activity scoring algorithm often used in pediatric and adolescent studies.
algorithms.description: Sadeh is frequently used in younger populations, though it is also applied more broadly in actigraphy workflows.
algorithms.citationText: Sadeh A, Sharkey KM, Carskadon MA. Activity-based sleep-wake identification.
algorithms.note: Favored for pediatric and adolescent applications.

algorithms.label: Oakley
algorithms.summary: Epoch-by-epoch rest/activity scoring algorithm associated with Actiware workflows.
algorithms.description: Oakley scoring is commonly associated with Actiware software from Respironics. Users should be cautious when applying it outside the device/software context it is commonly paired with.
algorithms.citationText: Oakley N. Validation with sleep/wake scoring in Actiwatch/Actiware contexts.
warnings: Use with caution on non-Respironics datasets.

parameterSchema.name: thresholdMode
parameterSchema.label: Threshold mode
parameterSchema.description: Run Oakley with automatically determined threshold, user-defined threshold, or both.

parameterSchema.name: manualThreshold
parameterSchema.label: Manual wake threshold
parameterSchema.description: Note. A value of 40 is commonly described as a medium wake threshold in Actiware-style contexts.

algorithms.label: Scripps
algorithms.summary: Epoch-by-epoch rest/activity scoring algorithm available in pyActigraphy’s sleep-algorithm toolkit.
algorithms.description: Scripps can be exposed alongside the other binary rest/activity scoring algorithms and previewed as an overlay over the full recording.
algorithms.citationText: Scripps Clinic / actigraphy sleep-wake scoring reference.
algorithms.note: Useful as an additional comparison overlay when users want to compare multiple scoring algorithms.

algorithms.label: Crespo
algorithms.summary: Pattern-based rest/activity detection with optional Crespo_AoT sleep-window estimation.
algorithms.description: Crespo is useful for identifying rest-activity transitions. Crespo_AoT estimates activity onset and offset times by detecting sustained low-activity/zero-activity structure and can derive no-diary rest windows.
algorithms.citationText: Crespo C, Aboy M, Fernández JR, Mojón A. Automatic identification of activity-rest periods based on actigraphy. Medical & Biological Engineering & Computing. 2012;50(4):329-340.

parameterSchema.name: mode
parameterSchema.label: Parameter mode
parameterSchema.description: Use pyActigraphy defaults or customize Crespo_AoT parameters.

parameterSchema.name: zeta
parameterSchema.label: zeta
parameterSchema.description: Maximum number of consecutive zeroes considered valid. Default: 15.

parameterSchema.name: zeta_r
parameterSchema.label: zeta_r
parameterSchema.description: Maximum number of consecutive zeroes considered valid during rest. Default: 30.

parameterSchema.name: zeta_a
parameterSchema.label: zeta_a
parameterSchema.description: Maximum number of consecutive zeroes considered valid during activity. Default: 2.

parameterSchema.name: t
parameterSchema.label: Invalid zero percentile
parameterSchema.description: Percentile for invalid zeroes. Default: 0.33.

parameterSchema.name: alpha
parameterSchema.label: Average sleep duration
parameterSchema.description: Expected average hours of sleep per night. Default: 8h.

parameterSchema.name: beta
parameterSchema.label: Padding sequence length
parameterSchema.description: Length of the padding sequence used during processing. Default: 1h.

parameterSchema.name: estimate_zeta
parameterSchema.label: Estimate zeta values
parameterSchema.description: Estimate zeta values from the distribution of consecutive-zero sequences. Default: false.

parameterSchema.name: seq_length_max
parameterSchema.label: Maximum sequence length
parameterSchema.description: Maximum length of random consecutive-zero series used when estimating zeta. Default: 100.

parameterSchema.name: verbose
parameterSchema.label: Print estimated zeta values
parameterSchema.description: If enabled, pyActigraphy prints estimated zeta values. Default: false.

algorithms.note: Crespo_AoT is generally sequence/rest-block based. It is a good starting option when users want pyActigraphy to estimate consolidated rest windows from activity data without a diary.

algorithms.label: Roenneberg
algorithms.summary: Trend-based automatic detection of consolidated sleep/rest episodes.
algorithms.description: Roenneberg_AoT estimates activity onset and offset times using a rolling activity trend and threshold-based detection of consolidated sleep episodes. It is more trend/consolidated-sleep oriented than Crespo.
algorithms.citationText: Roenneberg T, Keller LK, Fischer D, Matera JL, Vetter C, Winnebeck EC. Human Activity and Rest In Situ. Methods in Enzymology. 2015;552:257-283; Loock et al. Validation of the Munich Actimetry Sleep Detection Algorithm. Journal of Sleep Research. 2021.
warnings: pyActigraphy notes that Roenneberg_AoT was evaluated for actigraphy data aggregated in 10-minute bins. Use caution with other epoch lengths or resample appropriately.

parameterSchema.name: mode
parameterSchema.label: Parameter mode
parameterSchema.description: Use pyActigraphy defaults or customize Roenneberg_AoT parameters.

parameterSchema.name: trend_period
parameterSchema.label: Trend period
parameterSchema.description: Rolling window used to extract the activity trend. Default: 24h.

parameterSchema.name: min_trend_period
parameterSchema.label: Minimum trend period
parameterSchema.description: Minimum period required for the rolling trend window to produce a value. Default: 12h.

parameterSchema.name: threshold
parameterSchema.label: Threshold fraction
parameterSchema.description: Fraction of the trend used as the sleep/wake threshold. Default: 0.15.

parameterSchema.name: min_seed_period
parameterSchema.label: Minimum seed period
parameterSchema.description: Minimum period required to identify a potential sleep onset. Default: 30Min.

parameterSchema.name: max_test_period
parameterSchema.label: Maximum test period
parameterSchema.description: Maximum period of the test series. Default: 12h.

parameterSchema.name: r_consec_below
parameterSchema.label: Consecutive-below range
parameterSchema.description: Time range considered after the potential correlation peak. Default: 30Min.

parameterSchema.name: rsfreq
parameterSchema.label: Resampling frequency
parameterSchema.description: Optional resampling frequency. Leave blank for no resampling. Consider 10Min when matching the evaluation setting.

algorithms.note: Roenneberg_AoT is trend/consolidated-sleep based. It is useful for identifying the main nocturnal sleep/rest episode, especially when long-term activity trend is more informative than contiguous zero-activity blocks.
